title: Robotic surgical methods and apparatuses
abstract: Robotic surgical methods and apparatuses, including systems, for determining positioning information using a combination of AI landmark-identification and visual imaging. Also described herein are methods and apparatuses for determining how to train the AI. Also described herein are end effector devices that may be used with any of the robotic surgical methods and apparatuses. Also described herein designs and techniques incorporating AR into robotic surgical procedures.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method of operating a robotic surgical device, the method comprising: determining a position of one or more landmarks from an image of a first field of view of a camera associated with the robotic surgical device using a first neural network, further wherein the first neural network determines the depth of the one or more landmarks using an image of a second field of view that overlaps with the first field of view; and determining a surface normal for the one or more landmarks using a second neural network; and steering the robotic surgical device using the position and surface normal for the one or more landmarks. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising determining the position of a robotic arm of the robotic surgical device or of a device held in the robotic arm by triangulating between the image of the first field of view and the image of the second field of view. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the first and second neural networks operate in parallel. 4. The method of claim 1, further comprising determining the orientation of the one or more landmarks relative to the robotic arm using the surface normal and a camera angle of the camera. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the image of the first field of view and the image of the second field of view comprises arthroscopic images taken during the procedure. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein determining the position of the one or more landmarks comprises using a third neural network operating on one or more external scan images to detect a pre-planning position of the one or more landmarks and wherein the position of one or more landmarks comprises a combination of a position information from the first neural network and the pre-planning position from the third neural network. 7. The method of claim 1, further comprising detecting the one or more landmarks from the image of the first field of view of the camera using the first neural network. 8. The method of claim 6, further comprising detecting the one or more landmarks from the image of the first field of view of the camera using a combination of a putative landmark detection from the first neural network and landmark detecting from the third neural network. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein determining the surface normal for the one or more landmarks using the second neural network comprises using the image of the first field of view, the second field of view or both the first and second field of view. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein determining a surface normal for the one or more landmarks using the second neural network comprises combining surface normal information from the second neural network with surface normal information from a fourth neural network. 11. The method of claim 1, further comprising detecting pathological tissues from the first field of view and displaying an image of the image of the first field of view in which the pathological tissues are marked. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein detecting pathological tissues comprises determining from a plurality of CT and MRI scans a classification and location of pathological tissues on a 3D model. 13. The method of claim 1, wherein detecting one or more landmarks comprises receiving CT and MRI scans to create a patient specific 3D model of a joint. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein a first neural network is trained from an input dataset comprising a pre-procedure CT scan and/or MRI data. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the CT scan and/or MRI data are from a plurality of different subjects. 16. The method of claim 13, wherein the second neural network is trained from an input dataset of arthroscopic camera images from a plurality of different surgeries. 17. The method of claim 1, further comprising producing a bounding box around a pixel location of the one or more landmarks in each of the in the images of the first and second fields of view. 18. The method of claim 1, further comprising selecting two or more landmarks on which to train the first neural network, the second neural network, or both the first and second neural network, wherein at least one of the landmarks is selected to for having a feature with high variance and a second landmark is selected for having a feature with a low variance. 19. A method of operating a robotic surgical device, the method comprising: determining a position of one or more landmarks within an image of a first field of view of a camera associated with the robotic surgical device using a first neural network, further wherein the first neural network determines the depth of the one or more landmarks using an image of a second field of view that overlaps with the first field of view; and determining a surface normal for the one or more landmarks using a second neural network operating in parallel with the first neural network; and determining the orientation of the one or more landmarks relative to the robotic arm using the surface normal and a camera angle of the camera; and steering the robotic surgical device using the position and surface normal for the one or more landmarks. 20. A method of operating a robotic surgical device, the method comprising: detecting one or more landmarks within an image of a first field of view of a camera associated with the robotic surgical device and within an image of a second field of view that overlaps with the first field of view using an artificial intelligence (AI); and determining the position of a robotic arm or a device held in the robotic arm by triangulating between the image of the first field of view and the image of the second field of view. 21. The method of claim 20, further comprising detecting pathological tissues from the first field of view and displaying an image of the image of the first field of view in which the pathological tissues are marked. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein detecting pathological tissues comprises determining from a plurality of CT and MRI scans a classification and location of pathological tissues on a 3D model. 23. The method of claim 20, wherein detecting one or more landmarks comprises receiving CT and MRI scans to create a patient specific 3D model of a joint. 24. The method of claim 20, further comprising creating two separate neural networks to identify the one or more landmarks within the images of the first and second fields of view. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein a first neural network is trained from an input dataset comprising a pre-procedure CT scan and/or MRI data. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein the CT scan and/or MRI data are from a plurality of different subjects. 27. The method of claim 24, wherein the second neural network is trained from an input dataset of arthroscopic camera images from a plurality of different surgeries. 28. The method of claim 20, further comprising producing a bounding box around a pixel location of the one or more landmarks in each of the in the images of the first and second fields of view. 29. The method of claim 20, wherein determining the position of the robotic arm or the device held in the robotic arm by triangulating between the image of the first field of view and the image of the second field of view comprises reconstructing a depth of the landmark relative to the robotic arm or the device held in the robotic arm. 30. The method of claim 20, further comprising continuously refining the AI to recognize the landmark. 31. The method of claim 20, further comprising selecting two or more landmarks to train the AI on, wherein at least one of the landmarks is selected to for having a feature with high variance and a second landmark is selected for having a feature with a low variance. 32. The method of claim 20, further comprising continuously updating a database of optical images, MRI scans and CT scans used to train the AI. 33. The method of claim 20, further comprising coupling the robotic surgical device to a table on which a patient is positioned. 34. A method of operating a robotic surgical device, the method comprising: detecting one or more landmarks within an image of a first field of view of a camera associated with the robotic surgical device and within an image of a second field of view that overlaps with the first field of view using an artificial intelligence (AI); 

 producing a bounding box around a pixel location of the one or more landmarks in each of the in the images of the first and second fields of view; and determining the position of a robotic arm or a device held in the robotic arm by triangulating between the pixel location in each of the images of the first and second fields of view. 35. A system comprising: a robotic arm; one or more processors; and memory coupled to the one or more processors, the memory configured to store computer-program instructions, that, when executed by the one or more processors, perform a computer-implemented method comprising: determining a position of one or more landmarks from an image of a first field of view of a camera associated with the robotic surgical device using a first neural network, further wherein the first neural network determines the depth of the one or more landmarks using an image of a second field of view that overlaps with the first field of view; and determining a surface normal for the one or more landmarks using a second neural network; and steering the robotic surgical device using the position and surface normal for the one or more landmarks. 36. The system of claim 35, wherein the computer-implemented method further comprises determining the position of a robotic arm of the robotic surgical device or of a device held in the robotic arm by triangulating between the image of the first field of view and the image of the second field of view. 37. The system of claim 35, wherein the first and second neural networks operate in parallel. 38. The system of claim 35, wherein the computer-implemented method further comprises determining the orientation of the one or more landmarks relative to the robotic arm using the surface normal and a camera angle of the camera. 39. The system of claim 35, wherein the image of the first field of view and the image of the second field of view comprises arthroscopic images taken during the procedure. 40. The system of claim 35, wherein determining the position of the one or more landmarks comprises using a third neural network operating on one or more external scan images to detect a pre-planning position of the one or more landmarks and wherein the position of one or more landmarks comprises a combination of a position information from the first neural network and the pre-planning position from the third neural network. 41. The system of claim 35, wherein the computer-implemented method further comprises detecting the one or more landmarks from the image of the first field of view of the camera using the first neural network. 42. The system of claim 41, further comprising detecting the one or more landmarks from the image of the first field of view of the camera using a combination of a putative landmark detection from the first neural network and landmark detecting from the third neural network. 43. The system of claim 35, wherein determining the surface normal for the one or more landmarks using the second neural network comprises using the image of the first field of view, the second field of view or both the first and second field of view. 44. The system of claim 35, wherein determining a surface normal for the one or more landmarks using the second neural network comprises combining surface normal information from the second neural network with surface normal information from a fourth neural network. 45. The system of claim 35, wherein the computer-implemented method further comprises detecting pathological tissues from the first field of view and displaying an image of the image of the first field of view in which the pathological tissues are marked. 46. The system of claim 45, wherein detecting pathological tissues comprises determining from a plurality of CT and MRI scans a classification and location of pathological tissues on a 3D model. 47. The system of claim 35, wherein detecting one or more landmarks comprises receiving CT and MRI scans to create a patient specific 3D model of a joint. 48. The system of claim 47, wherein a first neural network is trained from an input dataset comprising a pre-procedure CT scan and/or MRI data. 49. The system of claim 48, wherein the CT scan and/or MRI data are from a plurality of different subjects. 50. The system of claim 47, wherein the second neural network is trained from an input dataset of arthroscopic camera images from a plurality of different surgeries. 51. The system of claim 35, further comprising producing a bounding box around a pixel location of the one or more landmarks in each of the in the images of the first and second fields of view. 52. The system of claim 35, further comprising selecting two or more landmarks on which to train the first neural network, the second neural network, or both the first and second neural network, wherein at least one of the landmarks is selected to for having a feature with high variance and a second landmark is selected for having a feature with a low variance. 53. A system comprising: a robotic arm; one or more processors; and memory coupled to the one or more processors, the memory configured to store computer-program instructions, that, when executed by the one or more processors, perform a computer-implemented method comprising: detecting one or more landmarks within an image of a first field of view of a camera associated with the robotic surgical device and within an image of a second field of view that overlaps with the first field of view using an artificial intelligence (AI); and determining the position of a robotic arm or a device held in the robotic arm by triangulating between the image of the first field of view and the image of the second field of view.
